course_title: Functional Anatomy of Locomotion & the Integument

learning_outcomes.description: Explain the basic principles of movement in vertebrates and identify the evolutionary influences on limb structure between species.

Compare and describe in detail the muscles, tendons, ligaments, bones, joints, nerves and blood vessels in the dog and horse, and more generally in the cow (ox) and pig, and understand the functional role of these anatomical structures in locomotion.
Discuss the specialised structures of the equine locomotory system including the stay apparatus, the hoof, and the shock absorbing mechanisms of equine limbs.
Appreciate the natural gaits of the dog and horse and how conformational defects in the horse may affect these gaits.

assessments.task_description:
This sequence includes 5 quizzes related to lecture materials that are held approximately after each anatomy block (see Blackboard for more information). Each quiz is worth 2% of the final course mark (10% in total). Each quiz will be made available for completion for one week (see Blackboard for dates), and will have a duration of 30 minutes and will be completed via Inspera.

Please check your course Blackboard site frequently for the latest information.
assessments.submission_guidelines: This demo highlights the most common questions and features and is designed to help you navigate your assessment in Inspera more easily. You may undertake the demo as many times as you wish. Your attempts are not tracked, and no responses will be saved or go towards your final grade. Please note, it is the student’s responsibility to ensure your exam browser is up to date prior to exams. Contact the campus library AskUs team for further assistance.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
This examination will consist of a mix of dissected dog cadavers, bones and pots. Each specimen will be at a separate station and students will progress from one station to the next spending 1.5 mins at each station. Each station usually has two or three questions on the material at that station. 

If practical exams cannot be held on campus, they may be conducted via Inspera on the student’s own laptop. Details about Inspera Assessment, including an introduction to Inspera functionality and where to go for support, are available. Further information will be provided on the Blackboard site for this course. The exam will incorporate a range of questions available in the Inspera examination platform.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
This examination will consist of a mix of horse limbs, bones, distal limb models and pots. Each specimen will be at a separate station and students will progress from one station to the next, spending 1.5 mins at each station. Each station usually has two or three questions on the material at that station. 

In the event that practical exams cannot be held on campus, the practical examination may be conducted via Inspera on the student’s own laptop. Details about Inspera Assessment, including an introduction to Inspera functionality and where to go for support, are available at Safe Exam Browser for Inspera - Library - University of Queensland (uq.edu.au). Further information will be provided on the Blackboard site for this course. The exam will incorporate a range of questions available in the Inspera examination platform.
